title: HTTP method semantics and idempotency
description: GET/PUT/DELETE are defined idempotent and POST is not; safe retries depend on this plus idempotency keys for POST.
body: Use when deciding whether a request is safe to retry. HTTP defines GET and HEAD as safe (no side effects) and GET/PUT/DELETE as idempotent (repeating yields the same server state), while POST is neither safe nor idempotent. A timeout is ambiguous: the request may have succeeded before the response was lost, so retrying a POST can duplicate the effect. Do not assume a failed-looking call did not happen on the server. Actions: prefer idempotent methods, and for POST send a client-generated idempotency key the server deduplicates on, or make the operation conditional (If-Match/ETag). Validation signals include a retried request with the same key applying exactly once. Risk boundary: retrying a non-idempotent request without dedup risks double charges/creates and is a correctness defect.
